mnemonics: P.I.E.S. - Parameters Interpret Effects: The coefficients (Parameters) in a regression equation Interpret the change in the dependent variable for a one-unit change in an independent variable, holding all others constant (their Effects).
keyPoints: Coefficients represent the expected change in the dependent variable for a one-unit change in the independent variable, assuming all other variables are held constant.
The intercept represents the expected mean value of the dependent variable when all independent variables are set to zero.
Interpretation depends on the type of variable (e.g., continuous, categorical) and the scale of the data (e.g., log-transformed).
commonMistakes: Interpreting a coefficient as the change caused by a variable without considering the 'all else held constant' assumption (ceteris paribus).
Interpreting coefficients on unscaled data as measures of importance or feature impact without considering the variables' units.
